sound_type: higher nasal call -- squeaky 'kak' or short high grunt at 800-5000 Hz, weaker and thinner than Snow Goose, higher-pitched, often lost in Snow Goose din, E=4 gentle coda, detected by voice in mixed flocks
ecosystem: Field, winter visitor. Agricultural fields and wetlands. Often mixed in Snow Goose flocks. Breeds on Arctic tundra (Queen Maud Gulf). North America's smallest goose.
season: Winter visitor (October-March). Breeds on Arctic tundra in summer. Mixed in Snow Goose flocks in PNW.
family: Anatidae (17th consecutive: 118-134 -- THE FINAL ANATIDAE! ALL-TIME RECORD CLOSES HERE!)

coordinates.description: Reference for Skagit Valley agricultural fields. Ross's Geese found mixed in Snow Goose flocks at Fir Island and surrounding areas.

taxonomy_note.description: SEVENTEENTH consecutive Anatidae (118-134) -- THE FINAL ENTRY! ALL-TIME RECORD. Third Anser genus entry. E=4 reflects the higher-pitched but lower-volume call of North America's smallest goose. Sixth and FINAL goose in the goose era (129-134). Named after Hudson's Bay Company factor Bernard Rogan Ross. The streak ENDS here. Degree 135 (Great Horned Owl) begins a new family.
taxonomy_note.current_family: Anatidae (17th and FINAL consecutive -- STREAK CLOSED)
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Anatidae: seventeen consecutive at degrees 118-134. The all-time record by a massive margin. Nearly doubled the Picidae record (9).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Anser from Latin 'goose.' rossii after Bernard Rogan Ross (1827-1874), Hudson's Bay Company chief factor at Fort Resolution, Great Slave Lake. Ross collected specimens shipped to the Smithsonian, where John Cassin described the species in 1861.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Anser -- third entry (after Greater White-fronted 132 and Snow 133). The gray geese. Some authorities have used Chen for Snow and Ross's, but AOS currently treats both as Anser.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Anseriformes. Seventeenth and FINAL consecutive Anatidae. The streak is CLOSED.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Anatidae at seventeenth consecutive and FINAL. The streak closes here. The all-time record. Next entry (Great Horned Owl, 135) begins a new family.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 800-5,000 Hz. The highest-pitched goose in the engine. Smaller body/syrinx produces higher fundamental frequency than Snow Goose (600-4,000).
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 1,800-4,000 Hz. Higher and squeakier than all other geese. The thin, nasal quality is distinctive.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Nasal call: 800-5,000 Hz. Individual squeak: one syllable. Alarm: sharper, more rapid.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual calls 0.2-0.8 seconds. One syllable. Short and clipped.
song_metrics.note_structure: HIGHER NASAL CALL: Squeaky 'kak' or short high grunt. Higher-pitched and weaker than Snow Goose. Thinner, more nasal quality. Simple waveform, less forceful delivery.
song_metrics.note_types: Contact call (primary, squeaky kak), alarm call (sharper), flight call (thin version of contact), feeding murmur (soft).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Short single calls with moderate gaps. In flocks, overlapping squeaks produce a higher-pitched chattering than Snow Goose flocks. Often masked by louder Snow Goose calls in mixed flocks.
song_metrics.timbre: Squeaky. Nasal. Thin. Higher than Snow Goose. Less forceful. The smallest goose produces the smallest sound.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Individual call is quiet and thin. Less flock amplification than Snow Goose (smaller independent flocks, often mixed). Gentle coda energy.
song_metrics.tempo: Moderate. Calls at 2-4 second intervals. Less continuous than Snow Goose flocks.
song_metrics.context: Flock communication, alarm, contact. Diagnostic in mixed Snow/Ross's flocks -- the higher pitch separates it. Voice as fastest detection cue for finding Ross's in Snow Goose flocks.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal variation. The call is largely innate.
song_metrics.key_property: HIGHER NASAL CALL -- E=4 GENTLE CODA. Squeaky 'kak' at 800-5,000 Hz. Highest-pitched goose. Weaker than Snow Goose. SEVENTEENTH CONSECUTIVE ANATIDAE (118-134) -- THE FINAL ENTRY. ALL-TIME RECORD CLOSED. North America's smallest goose. About 2/3 size of Snow Goose. Short stubby bill without grinning patch. Warty caruncles. Named after Bernard Rogan Ross (HBC). Population from ~6,000 to 1-2M+. Sixth and FINAL goose. The streak ends with the smallest, quietest goose of all.

plumage.overall: White morph (overwhelmingly dominant): entirely white with black wingtips. Short stubby bill, pinkish without black grinning patch. Blue morph extremely rare. Pink legs. Rounder, more compact head than Snow Goose.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Short stubby triangular bill WITHOUT grinning patch (diagnostic vs. Snow Goose). Warty caruncles at bill base in adult males. Smaller size (2/3 Snow Goose). Rounder head.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Juveniles: grayish wash, duller overall, no caruncles. Adults: clean white, pink bill, pink legs.
plumage.flight_pattern: Usually in flocks with Snow Geese. Noticeably smaller when side-by-side. Higher-pitched flight calls. White plumage identical to Snow Goose at distance -- size is the field separator.

physical_description.overall: Body length 53-66 cm (21-26 inches). Weight 1.0-1.6 kg (2.2-3.5 lbs). Wingspan 113-123 cm. North America's smallest goose. About 2/3 the length and half the weight of a Snow Goose.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Stubby bill without grinning patch. Warty caruncles (adult males). Rounder head. Significantly smaller than Snow Goose when compared directly in mixed flocks.
physical_description.field_id: In Skagit Valley Snow Goose flocks: scan for notably smaller white birds. Check bill: short, stubby, no grinning patch. Listen for higher-pitched squeaky call. Finding a Ross's in a Snow Goose flock is a satisfying birding challenge.

nodes.title: Higher Nasal Call -- E=4 Gentle Coda
nodes.description: Squeaky 'kak' at 800-5,000 Hz. Highest-pitched goose in the engine. Weaker and thinner than Snow Goose. Often lost in Snow Goose din. The streak ends with the smallest, quietest goose of all. E=4 as gentle conclusion.

nodes.title: Snow/Ross's Comparison Pair (133-134)
nodes.description: Mirrors Canada/Cackling (129-130): larger species (Snow, E=5) followed by smaller version (Ross's, E=4). Three comparison pairs in the goose era: swans (127-128), Branta (129-130), Anser (133-134). Structural elegance.

nodes.title: North America's Smallest Goose -- The Stubby Bill
nodes.description: About 2/3 Snow Goose size, half the weight. Short stubby triangular bill without grinning patch. Warty caruncles in adult males. Rounder head. Named after Bernard Rogan Ross (HBC, 1861). Population from ~6,000 to 1-2M+.

nodes.title: Goose Era COMPLETE (129-134) -- Six Geese, Six Sounds
nodes.description: Canada (129, deep honk, E=5), Cackling (130, higher cackle, E=4), Brant (131, low murmur, E=4), White-fronted (132, musical laugh, E=4), Snow (133, shrill bark, E=5), Ross's (134, nasal squeak, E=4). Six geese, two genera, three comparison pairs. COMPLETE.

nodes.title: Seventeenth Consecutive Anatidae -- THE FINAL ENTRY (118-134)
nodes.description: CLOSED. Seventeen entries. Ducks (9), swans (2), Branta geese (3), Anser geese (3). Three subfamilies, five+ genera. Nearly doubled the Picidae record (9). The all-time family streak record. Energy arc: opening to E=6 climax to E=4 coda. Degree 135 (Great Horned Owl) begins a new family.

cross_references.relationship: COMPARISON PAIR! Snow (133, E=5, shrill bark, massive flocks) to Ross's (134, E=4, nasal squeak, smaller). Mirrors Canada/Cackling (129-130). Large to small. The final comparison pair in the goose era.
PARALLEL PAIR! Cackling (130, E=4, smaller Canada) and Ross's (134, E=4, smaller Snow). Both are the diminutive version of the preceding species. Same energy, same structural pattern.
SEVENTEEN CONSECUTIVE -- CLOSED! Harlequin (118, E=5) to Ross's Goose (134, E=4). Seventeen consecutive Anatidae. The streak is CLOSED. The all-time record.
E=6 CLIMAX TO E=4 CODA! Trumpeter (128, E=6, the peak) to Ross's Goose (134, E=4, the quiet ending). The streak's full energy arc resolved.
NEW ERA! Ross's Goose (134, Anatidae, FINAL) to Great Horned Owl (135, Strigidae, NEW). The most dramatic family boundary since degree 118. Waterfowl to predator.
